core_concept: Counting to Ten and Ordinal Position
facts: The numeral '5' is the written symbol for the spoken number word 'five', and it always represents that many objects when counting a set.
When objects are placed in a line, the first object is the one at the very beginning, and the last object is the one at the very end, no matter how many objects are in between.
A bar graph shows data by using bars of different lengths; a bar that reaches the number 6 on the side means six objects were counted for that category.
The ordinal word 'first' means the object in position 1, not the object with the most or least of something.
misconceptions: A child counts the same object twice when counting a set of six objects because they do not track which objects have already been counted.
A child says the last object counted is 'first' because it is the one they touched last, confusing the order of touching with the ordinal position.
